message.content:
You are confirmer #1, independent. Confirm a production-hardening fix is BOTH appropriate (correct, no regression, idiomatic) AND complete (for a code change the named test must DISCRIMINATE — fail if reverted; for a doc/decision item, judge whether the documented claim is now accurate/honest). Repo /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay
Fix H-007 in scripts/license_gate.py
What: UNKNOWN/empty-license packages surfaced as ::warning:: (not silent); gate still fails only on deny matches.
Test(s): test_license_gate_surfaces_unknown_licenses
Read the actual changed file(s); read the test; check no regression. Verdict CONFIRMED (both true) or CONCERN. Be skeptical and specific.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
011942b fix(hardening-p5): batch B — reliability, CI, observability, docs (13 findings)
a9f3a48 fix(hardening-p5): batch A — methodology integrity / pre-registration firewall (H-001,002,003,015,016)
b483694 docs(hardening): pass-4 merged (#162); open pass-5 with retrospective dimensions
af29d19 Merge pull request #162 from thehipsterciso/harden/pass-4
1a61b89 docs(hardening): record pass-4 (23/24 confirmed, all fixed) + pass-5 dimensions
---
./scripts/license_gate.py

1	"""License gate (#147): fail if any installed dependency carries a strong-copyleft license.
2	
3	Reads a pip-licenses JSON report (path as argv[1], default ``licenses.json``) and exits non-zero
4	if any package's license matches a denied strong-copyleft family that would be incompatible with
5	the Apache-2.0 distribution. LGPL (weak copyleft, dynamic linking) is permitted. Advisory
6	monitoring of the rest is handled by Dependabot (.github/dependabot.yml).
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	import json
12	import sys
13	
14	# Strong-copyleft families denied in the distributed dependency set. Both the SPDX-style short
15	# tokens AND the full prose names must be listed (pass 3, #F-015): a package reporting
16	# "Server Side Public License" or "European Union Public Licence 1.2" contains neither the "SSPL"
17	# nor "EUPL" token, so the short tokens alone let those families bypass the gate.
18	_DENY = (
19	    "GPL",
20	    "AGPL",
21	    "SSPL",
22	    "EUPL",
23	    "SERVER SIDE PUBLIC",  # SSPL prose form
24	    "EUROPEAN UNION PUBLIC",  # EUPL prose form
25	    "AFFERO",  # AGPL prose form ("GNU Affero General Public License")
26	)
27	
28	
29	def main(path: str = "licenses.json") -> int:
30	    with open(path, encoding="utf-8") as f:  # context manager — no leaked fd (#F-050)
31	        packages = json.load(f)
32	    bad = []
33	    unknown = []
34	    for pkg in packages:
35	        raw = (pkg.get("License") or "").strip()
36	        lic = raw.upper()
37	        # A package whose license is empty / UNKNOWN / unspecified could be hiding a copyleft the
38	        # deny-substring scan can't see. Don't fail on it (pip-licenses reports UNKNOWN for many
39	        # legitimate packages with incomplete metadata), but SURFACE it as a CI warning so a
40	        # reviewer checks it instead of it passing silently (#H-007).
41	        if not raw or lic in ("UNKNOWN", "UNKNOWN LICENSE", "NONE"):
42	            unknown.append(f"{pkg.get('Name')} {pkg.get('Version')}")
43	            continue
44	        if "LGPL" in lic:  # weak copyleft — permitted for dynamically-linked libraries
45	            continue
46	        if any(tok in lic for tok in _DENY):
47	            bad.append(f"{pkg.get('Name')} {pkg.get('Version')}: {pkg.get('License')}")
48	    if unknown:
49	        print(
50	            "::warning::license gate: "
51	            + str(len(unknown))
52	            + " package(s) report no/UNKNOWN license — review for hidden copyleft: "
53	            + ", ".join(sorted(unknown))
54	        )
55	    if bad:
56	        print("::error::strong-copyleft licenses found:\n" + "\n".join(bad))
57	        return 1
58	    print(f"license gate OK ({len(packages)} packages checked, {len(unknown)} unknown)")
59	    return 0
60	
61	
62	if __name__ == "__main__":
63	    raise SystemExit(main(sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else "licenses.json"))
64

139:def test_license_gate_present():

173:def test_license_gate_behavioral_denies_strong_copyleft(tmp_path, license_name, denied):
178:    gate = _load_license_gate()

147	def _load_license_gate():
148	    import importlib.util
149	
150	    spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(
151	        "license_gate", _ROOT / "scripts" / "license_gate.py"
152	    )
153	    assert spec and spec.loader
154	    mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
155	    spec.loader.exec_module(mod)
156	    return mod
157	
158	
159\[REDACTED](
160	    "license_name,denied",
161	    [
162	        ("Server Side Public License", True),  # #F-015: SSPL prose form
163	        ("European Union Public Licence 1.2", True),  # #F-015: EUPL prose form
164	        ("GNU Affero General Public License v3", True),  # AGPL prose form
165	        ("SSPL", True),
166	        ("GPL-3.0", True),
167	        ("GNU Lesser General Public License v3 (LGPLv3)", False),  # weak copyleft permitted
168	        ("MIT", False),
169	        ("Apache-2.0", False),
170	        ("BSD-3-Clause", False),
171	    ],
172	)
173	def test_license_gate_behavioral_denies_strong_copyleft(tmp_path, license_name, denied):
174	    # #F-015 + #F-039: a BEHAVIORAL test (call main() on a synthetic report), not a string-presence
175	    # check — the prose-name SSPL/EUPL bypass and any logic regression are caught here.
176	    import json
177	
178	    gate = _load_license_gate()
179	    report = [{"Name": "pkg", "Version": "1.0", "License": license_name}]
180	    p = tmp_path / "licenses.json"
181	    p.write_text(json.dumps(report), encoding="utf-8")
182	    rc = gate.main(str(p))
183	    assert rc == (1 if denied else 0), f"{license_name!r}: expected denied={denied}"
184	
185	
186	def test_ci_test_lanes_install_hash_pinned_not_live_pypi():
187	    # #F-004: the core AND integration test lanes must install from the hashed lockfiles with
188	    # --require-hashes, not a live `pip install -e .[...]` against PyPI (where a malicious patch
189	    # release could execute under test).
190	    text = _ci_text()
191	    assert "--require-hashes -r requirements-core.lock" in text, (
192	        "core lane not hash-pinned (#F-004)"
193	    )
194	    assert "--require-hashes -r requirements.lock" in text, "integration lane not hash-pinned"
195	    assert (_ROOT / "requirements-core.lock").exists(), "requirements-core.lock missing (#F-004)"
196	    # the old live-resolution install of the engine-with-extras must be gone from the test lanes
197	    assert 'pip install -e ".[dev]"' not in text
198	    assert 'pip install -e ".[dev,reasoning' not in text
199	
200	
201	def test_core_lockfile_is_pinned_and_hashed_and_extra_free():
202	    # #F-004: the core lock backs the no-extras lane — it must be fully pinned + hashed and must
203	    # NOT pull in any backend extra (preserving the ADR-0006 dependency-free-core guard).
204	    core = (_ROOT / "requirements-core.lock").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
205	    assert "--hash=sha256:" in core
206	    for backend in ("arize-phoenix", "langgraph", "psycopg", "qdrant-client", "langchain-ollama"):
207	        assert f"\n{backend}==" not in core, f"core lock leaked a backend extra: {backend}"
208	
209	
210	def test_sbom_generation_fails_loud():
211	    # #F-012: the SBOM step must not mask pip-audit failure with `|| true`, and the upload must
212	    # error (not warn) on a missing file — a broken/absent SBOM cannot pass CI green.
213	    text = _ci_text()
214	    sbom_section = text[text.index("SBOM (CycloneDX)") :]
215	    assert "cyclonedx-json -o sbom.cyclonedx.json || true" not in sbom_section, "SBOM masks failure"
216	    assert "if-no-files-found: error" in text, "SBOM/license upload must error on missing file"
217	
218	
219	def test_license_gate_scans_pinned_lockfile_not_live_resolution():
220	    # #F-013: the license gate must scan the hash-pinned set, not a live `pip install -e .[all]`.
221	    text = _ci_text()
222	    gate_section = text[text.index("License gate") :]
223	    assert 'pip install -e ".[all]"' not in gate_section, "license gate still live-resolves .[all]"
224	    assert "--require-hashes -r requirements.lock" in gate_section
225	
226	
227	def test_ci_has_umbrella_required_check():
228	    # #F-014: a single fan-in job depends on every other job so branch protection requires only it
229	    # — audit/integration can't be silently dropped from the merge gate.
230	    text = _ci_text()
231	    assert "all-checks:" in text
232	    assert "needs: [core, integration, audit]" in text
233	
234	
235	def test_ci_pins_uv_version():
236	    # #F-031: uv must be pinned so a format change between versions cannot cause spurious stale-lock
237	    # diffs that block every PR.
238	    text = _ci_text()
239	    assert "pip install uv==" in text, "uv is not pinned in the lockfile sync check (#F-031)"
240	    assert "pip install --upgrade uv\n" not in text, "unpinned uv upgrade reintroduced"
241	
242	
243	def test_sbom_step_applies_the_allowlist():
244	    # #G-015: the SBOM pip-audit (which exits non-zero on ANY finding) must apply the same
245	    # allowlist as the gate, or an ACCEPTED advisory fails SBOM generation under set -euo pipefail
246	    # even though the gate passed.
247	    text = _ci_text()
248	    sbom = text[text.index("SBOM (CycloneDX)") : text.index("Upload SBOM artifact")]
249	    assert "--ignore-vuln" in sbom and ".pip-audit-ignore" in sbom, (
250	        "SBOM step does not apply the allowlist (#G-015)"
251	    )
252	    assert 'cyclonedx-json -o sbom.cyclonedx.json "${IGNORES[@]}"' in sbom
253	
254	
255	def test_audit_tooling_is_version_pinned():
256	    # #G-022: pip-audit and pip-licenses must be pinned so the supply-chain gate is reproducible
257	    # run-to-run (a tooling bump can't silently change advisory/license results).
258	    text = _ci_text()
259	    assert "pip-audit==" in text, "pip-audit not version-pinned (#G-022)"
260	    assert "pip-licenses==" in text, "pip-licenses not version-pinned (#G-022)"
261	    assert "install --upgrade pip pip-audit\n" not in text, (
262	        "unpinned pip-audit install reintroduced"
263	    )
264	
265	
266	def test_dependabot_lockfile_regen_is_documented():
267	    # #G-016: Dependabot can't regenerate the uv lockfiles, so the manual regen path must be
268	    # documented + scripted, or every Dependabot PR is stuck on the stale-lock gate.
269	    regen = _ROOT / "scripts" / "regenerate_lockfiles.sh"
270	    assert regen.exists(), "no scripts/regenerate_lockfiles.sh (#G-016)"
271	    body = regen.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
272	    assert "uv pip compile" in body and "requirements-core.lock" in body
273	    db = (_ROOT / ".github" / "dependabot.yml").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
274	    assert "regenerate_lockfiles.sh" in db, "dependabot.yml does not point at the regen script"
275	
276	
277	def test_license_gate_surfaces_unknown_licenses(tmp_path):
278	    # #H-007: an UNKNOWN/empty-license package must not pass SILENTLY (it could hide a copyleft) —
279	    # it is surfaced as a ::warning::; the gate still only FAILS on explicit deny matches.
280	    import json
281	
282	    gate = _load_license_gate()
283	    report = [
284	        {"Name": "mystery", "Version": "1", "License": "UNKNOWN"},
285	        {"Name": "blank", "Version": "1", "License": ""},
286	        {"Name": "ok", "Version": "1", "License": "MIT"},
287	    ]
288	    p = tmp_path / "licenses.json"
289	    p.write_text(json.dumps(report), encoding="utf-8")
290	    import io
291	    from contextlib import redirect_stdout
292	
293	    buf = io.StringIO()
294	    with redirect_stdout(buf):
295	        rc = gate.main(str(p))
296	    out = buf.getvalue()
297	    assert rc == 0  # unknown licenses do not FAIL the gate (would be too brittle)
298	    assert "::warning::" in out and "mystery" in out and "blank" in out  # but are surfaced
299	
300	
301	def test_ci_has_least_privilege_permissions():
302	    # #H-008: the workflow must pin the GITHUB_TOKEN to read-only at the top level.
303	    text = _ci_text()
304	    assert "permissions:" in text and "contents: read" in text, "no least-privilege token (#H-008)"
305	
306

content.text: The test discriminates. Let me confirm git state is clean after restore.

Verification complete.

**Appropriateness:** The fix is correct and idiomatic. `license_gate.py` collects empty/UNKNOWN/NONE-license packages into a separate `unknown` list (line 41 matches empty `raw`, plus `UNKNOWN`/`UNKNOWN LICENSE`/`NONE` on the uppercased value), `continue`s instead of failing, and emits a single `::warning::` GitHub Actions annotation listing them (sorted). The gate still returns 1 only on `_DENY` substring matches in `bad`. No regression: LGPL weak-copyleft still permitted, deny logic untouched, and the parametrized behavioral test (`test_license_gate_behavioral_denies_strong_copyleft`) still passes for all 9 cases. The empty-string `not raw` guard correctly catches blank licenses before the deny scan.

**Completeness:** The named test `test_license_gate_surfaces_unknown_licenses` discriminates. With the warning block reverted (silent pre-fix behavior), the test FAILS with `AssertionError: '::warning::' in 'license gate OK ...'`; restored, it passes. It asserts both that rc==0 (unknown does not fail the gate) and that `::warning::` plus both unknown package names (`mystery`, `blank`) are surfaced, while including an `ok`/MIT package to confirm normal packages aren't flagged.

File restored clean; git status unchanged.

input.reasoning: Read scripts/license_gate.py: UNKNOWN/empty/NONE-license packages are collected into a separate `unknown` list (line 41: matches empty `raw` plus UNKNOWN/UNKNOWN LICENSE/NONE uppercased), `continue`'d (not failed), and emitted as a single sorted `::warning::` GitHub annotation (lines 48-54). The gate still returns 1 only on `_DENY` substring matches (lines 55-57). Idiomatic, no regression — LGPL still permitted, deny scan untouched. Test test_license_gate_surfaces_unknown_licenses asserts rc==0 AND that `::warning::` plus both unknown package names (mystery, blank) appear in stdout, with an MIT package as a negative control. DISCRIMINATION VERIFIED: reverting the warning-print block makes the test fail (AssertionError: '::warning::' in 'license gate OK (3 packages checked, 2 unknown)'); restored it passes. Regression test test_license_gate_behavioral_denies_strong_copyleft passes all 9 parametrized cases (SSPL/EUPL/AGPL prose forms denied, LGPL/MIT/Apache/BSD permitted). File restored, git status clean.

content.text: Verified H-007 as CONFIRMED. Both appropriate and complete; test discriminates (fails on revert, passes when restored). File restored clean.
